zh.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I是Malo Laco de Mauley的兒子，於1190年出生在法國布列塔尼。他於大約1214年與Isabel de Thornham在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1236年被列為sheriff of northamptonshire。他去世於1241年在巴勒斯坦享年51歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I是Malo Laco de Mauley的儿子，于1190年出生在法国布列塔尼。他于大约1214年与Isabel de Thornham在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1236年被列为sheriff of northamptonshire。他去世于1241年在巴勒斯坦享年51岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mauley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Rusyn (from Slovakia) and Slovak Mašlej or Maslej, Polish Maślej, and probably also of Ukrainian Masliy: derivative of maslo (Polish masło) ‘butter’, applied as an occupational name for a dairyman or perhaps as a nickname (see Maslak ).

English: possibly a variant of Mansley with loss of -n-, itself perhaps a variant of Munslow, a habitational name from Munslow in Shropshire. The placename derives from an uncertain initial element + Old English hlāw ‘mound, hill’.

variants.name: Maslak
meanings: Polish and Jewish (from Poland) (Maślak): nickname from Polish maślak, the name of a species of edible fungus.

Slovak and Czech (Maslák); Polish and Jewish (from Poland) (Maślak); Ukrainian and Croatian; Rusyn (in Poland spelled Maślak): derivative of Slavic maslo (Polish masło) ‘butter’, applied as an occupational name for maker or seller of dairy produce, or perhaps as nickname for someone who was ‘as soft as butter’.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I was born in 1190, in Brittany, France as the son of Malo Laco de Mauley. He married Isabel de Thornham about 1214, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. In 1236, at the age of 46, his occupation is listed as sheriff of northamptonshire. He died in 1241, in Palestine, at the age of 51, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I est né en 1190, à Bretagne, France, fils de Malo Laco de Mauley. Il a épousé Isabel de Thornham environ 1214, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. En 1236, à l’âge de 46 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of northamptonshire. Il est décédé en 1241, à Palestine, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I wurde 1190 in Bretagne, Frankreich, als Sohn von Malo Laco de Mauley geboren. Er hat um 1214 in Yorkshire, England Isabel de Thornham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1236, als er 46 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of northamptonshire aufgeführt. Er starb 1241 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Palästina und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I è nato il nel 1190 nel Bretagne, Francia come figlio di Malo Laco de Mauley. Lui sposato Isabel de Thornham circa 1214, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1236, all'età di 46 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of northamptonshire. Morì nel 1241, nel Palestina, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley Iは1190年にフランスブレタグンでMalo Laco de Mauleyの息子として生まれた。約1214年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでIsabel de Thornhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1236年，46歳の時，職業はsheriff of northamptonshireと記録されている。彼は1241年にパレスチナで51歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I(은)는 1190년 프랑스 브레타그니에서 Malo Laco de Mauley의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1214년 영국 요르크실르에서 Isabel de Thornham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1236년, 46세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of northamptonshire이었다. 그는 1241년 팔레스타인에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I nasceu em 1190, em Bretagne, França filho de Malo Laco de Mauley. Ele casou-se com Isabel de Thornham aproximadamente 1214, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1236, com 46 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of northamptonshire. Ele faleceu em 1241, em Palestina, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I родился в 1190 г. в местности бретагн, Франция. Его отца звали Malo Laco de Mauley. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel de Thornham около 1214 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. В 1236 году, в возрасте 46 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of northamptonshire. Он умер в 1241 г. в местности Палестина в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Peter de Mauley I nació en 1190, en Bretagne, Francia como hijo de Malo Laco de Mauley. Se casó con Isabel de Thornham aproximadamente 1214, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1236, a la edad de 46 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of northamptonshire. Murió en 1241, en Palestina, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
